title: This star-forming galaxy is blowing out powerful winds topping 2 million mph
summary: NASA's XRISM X-ray spacecraft has measured winds exceeding 2 million mph emanating from a distant star-forming galaxy. This discovery highlights the intense processes driving galactic evolution.

title: Yes, NASA's launching Artemis 2 astronauts to the moon on April Fools' Day. It's not a joke.
summary: NASA is scheduled to launch the Artemis 2 mission, carrying astronauts to the moon, on April Fools' Day. The event is a serious milestone in space exploration, not a prank.

title: Comet MAPS faces a make-or-break moment as it dives toward the sun on April 4 — could it shine in the daytime sky?
summary: Comet MAPS is approaching a critical moment on April 4 as it passes extremely close to the sun, potentially becoming visible in the daytime sky. This close encounter at just 101,000 miles above the sun's surface will determine its fate.

title: What will happen if Artemis 2 astronauts get hit by a solar storm during NASA's ambitious moon mission?
summary: Artemis 2 astronauts could seek shelter in storage bays or near the toilet in the Orion capsule if a severe solar storm occurs during their moon mission. NASA has designated these areas as potential safe zones to minimize radiation exposure.

title: How to use your Unistellar smart telescope to track the Artemis 2 rocket light curve in the name of science
summary: Citizen scientists are using Unistellar smart telescopes to track the Artemis 2 rocket's light curve for planetary defense research. Unistellar's co-founder discussed this initiative with Space.com.

title: 'Forbidden Planet' at 70: Why the original sci-fi blockbuster is still out of this world
summary: The article celebrates the 70th anniversary of the sci-fi film 'Forbidden Planet', highlighting its cultural impact and influence on space opera genre. It notes how the movie reimagined Shakespeare's themes in a futuristic setting.

title: Here's what the Artemis 2 astronauts will be doing on each day of NASA's historic moon mission
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 mission, a 10-day historic moon mission, will involve science experiments, spacecraft tests, medical checks, and survival training for the crew. The astronauts will perform various tasks and activities during their lunar journey.

title: 'It didn't feel like a sci-fi movie': Esai Morales on bringing an Army Ranger to life in Netflix's sci-fi hit 'War Machine' (interview)
summary: Esai Morales discusses his role in Netflix's 'War Machine', highlighting the realism of portraying an Army Ranger. He reflects on how military characters often pay homage to family members.

title: Artemis 2 astronauts reveal adorable zero-g indicator 'Rise' | Space photo of the day for March 31, 2026
summary: The four astronauts of NASA's Artemis 2 mission arrived at Kennedy Space Center ahead of their historic moon mission, revealing an adorable zero-g indicator named 'Rise'. The mission is a significant step in NASA's lunar exploration program.

title: March of the Harmattan
summary: In March 2026, strong Harmattan winds carried Saharan dust across northwestern Africa and toward the Canary Islands, significantly reducing visibility and prompting weather alerts.

title: Godspeed, Artemis II!
summary: NASA astronaut Jessica Meir shared a photo of an Artemis program patch aboard the International Space Station, highlighting preparations for the upcoming Artemis II mission to return humans to the Moon.

title: Landsat Reveals Reservoir Changes and Bathymetry
summary: Researchers utilized Landsat data to uncover reservoir changes and bathymetry in two recent studies. This highlights the satellite's role in advancing our understanding of water bodies.

title: Sendoff for Artemis II Crew
summary: NASA and CSA astronauts pose for a photo before the Artemis II crew's media event. The crew includes primary and backup members for the upcoming Artemis II mission.

title: Seeing Blue During Schirmacher’s Summer Melt Season
summary: A network of meltwater lakes and drainage channels has turned the Antarctic ice shelf known for its blue ice areas even bluer during the summer melt season. This phenomenon highlights the impact of climate change on polar regions.

title: I Am Artemis: Erik Richards
summary: Erik Richards, Near Space Network Mission Manager, discusses his career supporting NASA's Artemis mission, which marks the culmination of his lifelong dream tied to spaceflight and communication with spacecraft.

title: NASA Selects Intuitive Machines to Deliver Artemis Science, Tech to Moon
summary: NASA has selected Intuitive Machines for a $180.4 million contract to deliver science and technology payloads to the Moon under the CLPS initiative and Artemis program. The mission includes seven payloads, five of which are NASA-funded, to enhance lunar surface research.

title: NASA’s Environment and Energy “Blue Marble” Awards Categories
summary: NASA announced the 'Blue Marble' Awards categories, including the Environmental Quality Award and the Award for Excellence in Project or Program Execution. These awards recognize achievements in environmental management, conservation, and project execution.

title: NISAR’s View of Mount Rainier
summary: NISAR, a U.S.-Indian Earth observation satellite, captured an image of Mount Rainier on November 10, 2025, using its L-band SAR instrument to penetrate cloud cover. The satellite's data provides detailed surface observations of the Pacific Northwest region.

title: Artemis II set to return humanity to the Moon with launch on Wednesday evening
summary: Artemis II is scheduled to launch on Wednesday evening to return humans to the Moon, marking a significant milestone in space exploration. The mission follows the historic Apollo 17 completion in 1972.

title: Launch Preview: SLS, Falcon 9, Atlas V, and Soyuz launches comprise busy launch manifest
summary: Nine launches are scheduled this week, featuring rockets like SLS, Falcon 9, Atlas V, and Soyuz lifting off from Florida, California, and Russia.

title: America’s Rocket Factory comes Full Circle with Artemis
summary: Rocket Factory, a key player in America's space industry, is closely involved with NASA's Artemis program, marking a significant milestone in the return of crewed launches beyond low Earth orbit. The collaboration highlights the growing role of private companies in advancing deep-space missions.

title: SpaceX delivers 119 payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit on Transporter 16
summary: SpaceX successfully launched 119 payloads to a Sun-synchronous orbit as part of the Transporter 16 mission from California. This marks another milestone in SpaceX's rideshare program, demonstrating its capability to deploy multiple small satellites in a single launch.

title: NASA outlines Moon Base plans, pivots on Gateway
summary: NASA announced detailed plans for a Moon base during its 'Ignition' event, pivoting away from its previous Gateway lunar outpost focus. This marks a significant shift in the agency's lunar exploration strategy.

title: Isar Aerospace scrubs second launch of Spectrum rocket
summary: Isar Aerospace, a European private space launch provider, has scrubbed the second launch attempt of its Spectrum rocket. The company, based in Ottobrunn, Germany, had planned the launch as part of its commercial spaceflight efforts.

title: NASA unveils Space Reactor-1 Freedom mission to Mars in 2028
summary: NASA announced the Space Reactor-1 Freedom mission to Mars, planned for 2028, with Administrator Jared Isaacman and other leaders detailing the initiative.

title: Progress on Starbase Pads ahead of Block 3 Starships
summary: SpaceX is making significant progress on upgrades to Starbase launch pads to support the upcoming Block 3 Starships, aiming to increase launch frequency. This effort highlights SpaceX's focus on enhancing operational readiness at its Texas facility.

title: Launch Preview: Russia to debut Soyuz-5; Falcon 9 and Atlas V to launch internet satellites
summary: Russia is set to debut its new Soyuz-5 rocket, while the U.S. will launch internet satellites using Falcon 9 and Atlas V rockets. Multiple countries, including the U.S., Russia, China, New Zealand, and Norway, are preparing for upcoming launches.

title: Progress MS-33 launches to ISS from repaired pad
summary: Progress MS-33 successfully launched to the International Space Station (ISS) from the repaired Baikonur Cosmodrome pad. The launch follows repairs made to the launch site after the previous Soyuz MS-28 mission.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website reported on space industry developments without specifying details.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary with a booklet highlighting 50 key achievements. The publication marks five decades of milestones in the European space sector.

title: Proba-3’s Coronagraph is alive!
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft has re-established contact with ground control after a month-long communication loss due to an anomaly. This marks a significant recovery for the mission team.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting events to present the follow-up to the CM25 meeting, detailing planned activities and industrial opportunities across its programs. The events aim to facilitate direct exchange between ESA and the industrial community.

title: Plato readies for space-like tests
summary: ESA's Plato exoplanet-hunting mission is undergoing critical space-like tests in the Large Space Simulator chamber, simulating extreme temperatures and vacuum conditions. The spacecraft, equipped with 26 ultrasensitive cameras, is set for a January 2027 launch on an Ariane 6 rocket.

title: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visits ESTEC
summary: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visited ESTEC, the European Space Agency's technical center. This visit highlights international collaboration in space exploration.

title: ESA rolls out new costing software for its suppliers
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will launch ECOS version 6 on March 30, 2026, making it mandatory for all suppliers to use this new costing software in their financial proposals for ESA tender actions.

title: The tiny lens antenna with big potential
summary: A PhD researcher has developed a compact, 3D-printed lens antenna to enhance satellite navigation resilience against interference, offering a cost-effective alternative to bulky traditional antennas.

title: Work ongoing to restore contact with Proba-3's Coronagraph
summary: An anomaly on Proba-3's Coronagraph spacecraft caused a loss of contact with ground control. Mission teams are investigating the issue and working to restore communication.

title: Students kick off experiments on ESA’s flat floor
summary: Students have begun conducting experiments on ESA's flat floor facility, which simulates low-gravity environments for testing robotic systems. This initiative aims to advance space technology research and education.

title: Top Space Lawmaker on Moon Base, Artemis Plans
summary: A top space lawmaker has expressed strong support for establishing a Moon base as a strategic step towards future Mars missions. The comments highlight growing enthusiasm for NASA's Artemis program and long-term lunar exploration plans.

title: Voyager and Icarus Team Up on ISS Robotics Demo
summary: Voyager Space and Icarus Robotics are collaborating on a robotics demonstration aboard the International Space Station (ISS), marking a significant step in space-based automation. This partnership highlights the growing role of private companies in advancing ISS capabilities.

title: Q&A With Lunar Base Manager Carlos Garcia-Galan
summary: Carlos Garcia-Galan transitioned from managing NASA's lunar-orbiting habitat program to overseeing the agency's plan for a rapid lunar surface base construction. He was interviewed during NASA HQ's Ignition event.

title: Transporter-16 Sends 119 Payloads to Orbit
summary: The Transporter-16 mission successfully launched 119 payloads to orbit. This marks another 'rideshare' mission delivering multiple small satellites for various customers.

title: Starcloud Raises $170M Series A at $1.1B Valuation
summary: Starcloud has raised $170 million in a Series A funding round, valuing the company at $1.1 billion. This investment underscores strong investor interest in orbital data-center ventures and Starcloud's proven flight heritage.

title: Bellatrix Raises $20M For In-Space Propulsion Tech
summary: Bellatrix Aerospace raised $20 million for its in-space propulsion technology. The funds will support scaling production infrastructure after flight-qualifying core technologies.

title: Xona Space Systems Raises $170M Series C to Scale Private GPS
summary: Xona Space Systems has raised $170 million in a Series C funding round to expand its private GPS capabilities. The company aims to provide stronger, more accurate, and secure PNT signals in Low Earth Orbit (LEO) compared to traditional GPS.

title: Jared Isaacman Hits 100 Days In Office With a Bang
summary: Jared Isaacman, NASA Administrator, marked 100 days in office by emphasizing an ambitious goal to return to the Moon. The statement underscores a renewed focus on lunar exploration under his leadership.

title: With Artemis Changes, Europe is Left Holding the Bag
summary: ESA and European space primes invested heavily in components for the lunar orbiting station, but recent Artemis program changes have left them uncertain about future roles. Europe may now bear the financial and technical burden without clear participation in the revised Artemis plans.

title: Arkadia Space Secures €14.5M to Develop New Biprop Engine
summary: Arkadia Space, a Spanish propulsion startup, secured €14.5 million from the European Investment Council to develop a new bipropellant engine.

title: Faced With Starlink Competition, Ground Segment Leaders Turn to Orchestration, Specialization
summary: Ground segment providers are adapting to competition from LEO megaconstellations like Starlink by focusing on orchestration and specialization. This shift is driven by the need to innovate in response to vertically integrated satellite networks.

title: Delta Taps Amazon Leo for IFC Rollout on 500 Aircraft
summary: Delta Air Lines has partnered with Amazon to deploy Amazon Leo's in-flight connectivity (IFC) service on 500 aircraft starting in 2028. This collaboration marks a significant expansion of Delta's connectivity offerings.

title: Air Force Awards BlackSky $99M to Large Aperture Optical Payload for Space-Based Imaging
summary: The U.S. Air Force awarded BlackSky a $99 million contract to accelerate the design and development of a large aperture optical payload for space-based imaging. The contract was received in early March from the Air Force Research Laboratory.

title: Claussen Departs, ST Engineering iDirect Names Sridhar Kuppanna as CEO
summary: ST Engineering iDirect has appointed Sridhar Kuppanna as its new CEO, succeeding Don Claussen who led the company for three years. The leadership change marks a transition in the company's executive team.

title: Es’hailSat and EgyptSat Sign VSAT Partnership Deal
summary: Es’hailSat and EgyptSat have signed a multi-year VSAT partnership deal to enhance connectivity services in the Middle East. The agreement aims to leverage the strengths of both satellite companies for regional improvements.

title: Starcloud Raises $170M to Fund Orbital Data Center Plans
summary: Starcloud, a space startup, raised $170 million in a Series A funding round to develop orbital data centers using satellites. The company is now valued at $1.1 billion.

title: WAVE Achieves First Cloud-to-Gateway Satcom Virtualization with AI Signal Analysis
summary: The WAVE Consortium, an IEEE standards body, achieved the first standardized waveform virtualization using FPGA acceleration in a cloud-to-gateway satellite communication (Satcom) system with AI signal analysis.

title: SpaceX Launches 119 Payloads on the Transporter-16 Rideshare Mission
summary: SpaceX successfully launched 119 payloads into Low-Earth Orbit (LEO) on the Transporter-16 rideshare mission. The launch took place at 4:02 a.m. PT from Vandenberg Space Force Base in California.

title: Live in the Booth: AST SpaceMobile President Scott Wisniewski Talks Spectrum Strategy and Defense Potential
summary: AST SpaceMobile President Scott Wisniewski discusses the company's spectrum strategy and defense potential during SATShow Week, highlighting the recent launch of the BlueBird 6 satellite.

title: SpaceBridge Launches UniHub as Streamlined VSAT Platform
summary: SpaceBridge launched UniHub, a compact all-in-one VSAT hub platform designed to simplify satellite network deployment with high performance, efficiency, and scalability.
